intro: Corunastylis ectopa, commonly known as the eastern bronzewing orchid, is a species of flowering plant in the orchid family. This plant has been traditionally used in Australian Aboriginal medicine, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in treating skin conditions and wound healing. The bioactive constituents of Corunastylis ectopa include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which have been identified as responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea or infused oil, and has also been used to make topical creams and ointments for skin issues.
